name: Pierre Michelot
abstract: Pierre Michelot (3 March 1928 – 3 July 2005) was a French jazz double bass player and arranger.

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: They were the trio. Miles played with that trio, and Lester played with that trio, rhythm section. The Quartet played its stuff. That was the Birdland All-Stars and we toured by bus.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Kenny Clarke ... formed an orchestra, and I joined it. ... In the orchestra was ... Pierre Michelot, a fine French bass player

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: we had a French bass player, Monsieur Pierre Michelot... everywhere we went man in Europe
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: So Henri set it up so that I would be the drummer and that Pierre Michelot (PHONETIC) would be the bassist.

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Tadd Dameron. Pierre Michelot. [INTERVIEWEE] No, he didn’t [unintelligible/overlapping].
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Michelot? Not him? Michelot? >> [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Pierre, yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
